title: MD
biography: Albert Sabin was a Polish-American virologist who developed the oral polio vaccine (OPV), which became the dominant form of polio immunization worldwide due to its ease of administration and ability to induce intestinal immunity. Born in Bialystok, Poland, he immigrated to the United States in 1921 and also made significant contributions to research on dengue fever, encephalitis, and toxoplasmosis.
aiSummary: Albert Sabin was a virology and vaccine development specialist with an H-index of 40 at the University of Cincinnati College of Medicine. Developed the oral polio vaccine (OPV) that became the dominant vaccine used in global polio eradication efforts. Based in United States.
techniquesInvented: Live attenuated poliovirus strains for vaccination
education: New York University School of Medicine (MD, 1931)

timeline.title: Proved that poliovirus enters the body through the digestive tract, not the nose
Began large-scale human trials of oral polio vaccine
Oral polio vaccine licensed for use in the United States
Named president of the Weizmann Institute of Science in Israel
